**Candlewick** is a small ward,[[1]](#cite_note-brithist-1) one of the [25 ancient wards](/wiki/Wards_of_the_City_of_London "Wards of the City of London") in the [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), England.

It was named after [Cannon Street](/wiki/Cannon_Street "Cannon Street"), which historically was called Candlewright (or Candlewick) Street,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and a small part of Cannon Street continues to be within the ward's boundaries.

Its northern boundary runs along [Lombard Street](/wiki/Lombard_Street,_London "Lombard Street, London") – to the north is the ward of [Langbourn](/wiki/Langbourn "Langbourn"). [Gracechurch Street](/wiki/Gracechurch_Street "Gracechurch Street") forms Candlewick's eastern boundary with [Bridge](/wiki/Bridge_(City_of_London_ward) "Bridge (City of London ward)") ward, down to the [Monument to the Great Fire of London](/wiki/Monument_to_the_Great_Fire_of_London "Monument to the Great Fire of London"), erected to commemorate the place where the [Great Fire](/wiki/Great_Fire_of_London "Great Fire of London") started 2 September 1666. Its southern boundary curves along Arthur Street, incorporating traffic from [London Bridge](/wiki/London_Bridge "London Bridge") to its western edge along Laurence Pountney Lane, Sherbourne Lane and Abchurch Lane in [Walbrook](/wiki/Walbrook "Walbrook") ward.

There are two churches within Candlewick, [St. Mary Abchurch](/wiki/St_Mary_Abchurch "St Mary Abchurch") on Abchurch Lane and [St. Clement Eastcheap](/wiki/St_Clement_Eastcheap "St Clement Eastcheap") on Clement's Lane,[[1]](#cite_note-brithist-1) while a third, [St. Michael, Crooked Lane](/wiki/St._Michael,_Crooked_Lane "St. Michael, Crooked Lane"), was demolished in 1831 to make way for the new London Bridge.[[3]](#cite_note-3) There are several large stores and pubs and a hotel located in the ward. As with many City wards it has its own social club and newsletter.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[Monument tube station](/wiki/Bank-Monument_station "Bank-Monument station") is located in the south-eastern corner of the ward.

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Candlewick_(ward)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Politics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3a/Ward_of_Candlewick_2015.jpg/250px-Ward_of_Candlewick_2015.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ward_of_Candlewick_2015.jpg)

Ward plaque

Candlewick is one of 25 wards in the [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London"), each electing an [alderman](/wiki/Alderman "Alderman") to the [Court of Aldermen](/wiki/Court_of_Aldermen "Court of Aldermen") and commoners (the City equivalent of a [councillor](/wiki/Councillor "Councillor")) to the [Court of Common Council](/wiki/Court_of_Common_Council "Court of Common Council") of the [City of London Corporation](/wiki/City_of_London_Corporation "City of London Corporation"). Only electors who are [Freemen of the City](/wiki/Freedom_of_the_City "Freedom of the City") are eligible to stand.

The Ward is represented on the Court of Alderman by [Alderman Professor Emma Edhem](/wiki/Emma_Edhem "Emma Edhem"),[[5]](#cite_note-5) and on the Court of Common Council by Christopher Paul Boden and James St John Davis.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
